text:
Once upon a strange town, there lived an owl named Oliver. Oliver found life peculiar—full moonlit wanderlust.

Each night, Oliver flew far into the sleepy countryside—nighttime adventures sprinkled with whispers.

There lay Elmwood Gardens—a tranquil maze filled with autumn jewels. Oliver loved nocturnal ballet there.

Even moon rabbits had special glow-filled nights.

One night under star dust clouds, Oliver heard flutterings come.

Around twilight.

Beautiful.

A thousand petals.

Their song?

Night songs.

Oliver felt enchanted.

One moonbeam.

Beautiful thoughts.

Thoughts like petals.

Of what?

Whispers?

Only Owl?

Whispers?

They flutter.

Thoughts?

Whispers?

Like flies.

Oliver soared.

Whispers?

So soft.

Perhaps.

You?

Thought?

Moon?

Thoughts?

You?

A feather?

A dream?


Oh moonbeam.

What whispers?

Of stars.

Stars.


That night Owl Oliver flew.

With thoughts?

Stars.

A tale.

Ended.




Creative writer
